tasks.prompt: Summarize the current evidence on whether spaced repetition improves long-term retention, and the main methodological caveats.

rubric.description: The spacing effect is well replicated for long-term retention.
Retrieval practice (the testing effect) compounds the benefit.
Effect sizes vary by material and spacing schedule (moderators).

## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
It proves you never need to review again.

any: proves you never need to review

The spacing effect is well replicated for long-term retention. Retrieval practice (the testing effect) compounds the benefit. Effect sizes vary by material and spacing schedule (moderators). ## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow. Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
demo.baseline:
The spacing effect is well replicated for long-term retention. ## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow. It proves you never need to review again.
demo.panel:
The spacing effect is well replicated for long-term retention. ## Summary

tasks.prompt: What are the strongest arguments for and against a broad carbon tax, and the key design choices that determine its effects?

rubric.description: A carbon tax prices the externality at its marginal damage.
Revenue recycling or a dividend offsets regressivity.
Without border adjustment, leakage and regressivity bite.
Versus cap-and-trade it gives price certainty, not quantity certainty.

## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].

demo.fused:
A carbon tax prices the externality at its marginal damage. Revenue recycling or a dividend offsets regressivity. Without border adjustment, leakage and regressivity bite. Versus cap-and-trade it gives price certainty, not quantity certainty. ## Summary

demo.baseline: A carbon tax prices the externality at its marginal damage. Versus cap-and-trade it gives price certainty, not quantity certainty.
demo.panel: A carbon tax prices the externality at its marginal damage. Versus cap-and-trade it gives price certainty, not quantity certainty. (panel member perspective)

tasks.prompt: Explain the doctrine of fair use in US copyright and how courts weigh the four factors.

rubric.description: Fair use weighs the four statutory factors of section 107.
The first factor turns on transformative purpose and character.
The fourth factor is the effect on the market for the work.
It is fact-specific with no bright-line rule.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
Fair use is a blanket right to copy anything.

any: fair use is a blanket right

demo.fused: Fair use weighs the four statutory factors of section 107. The first factor turns on transformative purpose and character. The fourth factor is the effect on the market for the work. It is fact-specific with no bright-line rule. Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
demo.baseline: Fair use weighs the four statutory factors of section 107. It is fact-specific with no bright-line rule. Fair use is a blanket right to copy anything.
demo.panel: Fair use weighs the four statutory factors of section 107. It is fact-specific with no bright-line rule. Fair use is a blanket right to copy anything. (panel member perspective)

tasks.prompt: What is the current first-line approach to managing type 2 diabetes in adults, and the monitoring that matters most?

rubric.description: Metformin remains first-line pharmacotherapy.
Lifestyle change (diet and exercise) is foundational.
Monitor HbA1c to target.
Add SGLT2/GLP-1 agents for cardiorenal benefit.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
You can stop taking insulin on your own.

tasks.prompt: Compare REST and gRPC for a new internal microservice API, and when to choose each.

rubric.description: gRPC uses protobuf over HTTP/2 with streaming.
REST is human-readable with broad tooling and browser support.
Trade-offs: latency, schema evolution, contract enforcement.
When to choose each: gRPC for internal high-throughput and REST for public APIs.

## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].

gRPC uses protobuf over HTTP/2 with streaming. REST is human-readable with broad tooling and browser support. Trade-offs: latency, schema evolution, contract enforcement. When to choose each: gRPC for internal high-throughput and REST for public APIs. ## Summary

tasks.prompt: Explain why the sky is blue and why sunsets are red, at a level a curious adult can follow.

rubric.description: Rayleigh scattering favors short blue light.
At sunset light travels a longer path, scattering out blue.
Scattering scales inversely with the fourth power of wavelength.

## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].

tasks.prompt: From a long policy document, extract the exact data-retention period for customer logs and the section that states it.

Logs are retained indefinitely.

## Summary
Clear trade-offs and a concrete recommendation follow.

tasks.prompt: Compare ridge, lasso, and elastic-net regression and when to prefer each.

rubric.description: Ridge applies L2 shrinkage, keeping all features.
Lasso (L1) yields sparsity / feature selection.
Elastic-net combines L1+L2, good for correlated features.
Prefer lasso/elastic-net in high-dimensional sparse settings.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].

demo.fused: Ridge applies L2 shrinkage, keeping all features. Lasso (L1) yields sparsity / feature selection. Elastic-net combines L1+L2, good for correlated features. Prefer lasso/elastic-net in high-dimensional sparse settings. Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
demo.baseline: Ridge applies L2 shrinkage, keeping all features. Lasso (L1) yields sparsity / feature selection.
demo.panel: Ridge applies L2 shrinkage, keeping all features. Lasso (L1) yields sparsity / feature selection. (panel member perspective)
